sutta: buddha said monks there are these three deva messengers what three in this connection a certain one lives an immoral life indeed word and thought so doing when body breaks up after death he is reborn in the waste the wolf who planes the downfall in hell then monks the waters of hell seize him by both arms and bring him before yama the lord saying saya this man had no respect for mother and father recluses and brahmins he showed no respect to the elders of his clan let your majesty inflict due punishment on him 10 monks yama the lord examines him closely questions him and addresses him concerning the first deva messenger saying now my good man have you never seen the first deva messenger manifest among men and he replies i have not seen him zaya then says king yama to him what my good man have you never seen any human being a woman or a man 80 or 90 or 100 years of age broken down bent inwards like the rafter of a roof crooked propped up with a cane and trembling as he goes along an ailing creature past his prime with broken teeth gray head or bowless or hairless bald with wrinkled brow and limbs all blotched and spotted then the man says sire i have sinned then says yama lord of death to him my good man did it never occur to you as a man of intelligence and fully grown i too am subject to old age i have not overpassed old age come let me act nobly indeed word and thought then the other says no sire i could not i was negligent ten monks yama lord of death says to him my good man it was true negligence that you did not act nobly in deed word and thought truthfully they shall do unto you in accordance with your negligence that evil action of yours was not done by mother father brother sister friends and comrades not by kinsmen devas recluses and brahmins by yourself alone was it done it is just you that will experience the fruit thereof ten monks yama lord of death having examined him closely questioned and addressed him concerning the first deva messenger thus likewise concerning the second deva messenger saying my good man have you never seen the second deva messenger manifest among men and he replies i have never seen him messiah then says yama lord of death to him what my good man have you never seen among human beings a woman or a man sick afflicted suffering from a sore disease lie wallowing in his own filth that's excrement by some lifted up by others put to bed and he says sire i have seen then king yama says then my good man did it never occur to you as a man of intelligence and fully grown i too am subject to disease i have not overpassed disease come let me act nobly indeed word and thought then says he sire i could not i was negligent then says yama lord of death to him my good man it was true negligence that you did not act nobly indeed word and thought merely they shall do unto you according to your negligence that evil action of yours was not done by mother father brother sister friends and comrades etc by yourself alone was it done it is just you that will experience the fruit thereof there upon monks yama lord of death having examined him closely concerning the second deva messenger just like why is concerning the third deva messenger saying my good man have you never seen among human beings a woman or a man a corpse one two or three days dead swollen black and blue and festering and he replies sire i have seen and kiniyama says then my good man did it never occur to you as a man of intelligence and fully grown i too am subject to death i have not overpassed death come let me act nobly indeed word and thought then say is he sire i could not i was negligent and yama lord of death says to him my good man it was true negligence that you did not act nobly indeed word and thought verily they shall do unto you according to your negligence that evil action of yours was not done by mother father etc etc by yourself alone was it done it is just you that will experience the fruit thereof ten monks having examined him closely concerning the third deva messenger yama lord of death is silent thereupon monks the waters of hell torture him with the fivefold pinion they drive a hot iron pin through each hand and foot and the fifth through the middle of his breasts there at he suffers grievous violent sharp and bitter agonies but he makes not an end until that evil deed of his has been worked out then the warders of hell lay him down and playing him down with access there and he suffers bitter agonies till that evil deed of his has been worked out next they place him with feet up and head down and playing him with razors then they bind him to a chariot and drive him up and down over a blazing ground flaming and all the glow there he suffers bitter uh grievous violent sharp and bitter agonies then they push him up and down a huge burning mountain of red hot cold blazing flaming and all the glow and he suffers again grievous violent sharp and bitter agonies but he makes not an end until that evil deed of his has been worked out then they take him feet up and hit down and plunge him into a burning brazen cauldron blazing flaming and all aglow there he is cooked and rises to the surface with the scum so doing once he comes up and once he goes down and once he goes across there and he suffers agonies grievous violence sharp and bitter yet he makes not an end until that evil deed of his has been worked out thereupon monks the waters of hell toss him into the great hell now monks this great help is described thus four square the great health standard with four gates divided and partitioned with the wall of iron gird of iron is the roof its floor of iron two dazzling and hot and flashing all around 100 leagues stands fast forever immovable once upon a time monks yama lord of death thought thus to himself true it is i think that they who in the world do evil deeds in diverse ways does suffer retribution oh that oh if only i could win birth as a human being if only a tatar gatherer were born into the world and arahan who is a fully enlightened one if only i might sit at the feet of that exalted one and then that exalted one would teach me dhamma and then i might learn dhamma from the exalted one now monks i see this not hearing it from some recluse or brahmins but what i myself have known and seen and heard that do i declare unto you so this
commentary: is uh also quite uh interesting suta where we talk about king yama yen uh it appears from the sutas that there are many hells like hells is like a region you know and each region of hell now is great hell there is a king and that king is called king yama so of the various hells yeah there are various kings and the king yama is addressed as deva by the hell beings you know because he is in a much better condition than the other hell beings so they call him deva so when they when they mentioned here the three deva messengers it might either be the three heavenly messengers or the three messengers from hell and you can see from here the type of suffering that a person undergoes in hell there are some other sutas which also mention something like this but even worse where they suffer from one hell and they suffer all kinds of tortures and then they are like a fugitive you know trying to run away because when they are caught and they are tortured by these beings in hell so they are running trying to avoid these beings in hell running running and once in a long while the gates of that hell will open once in a long while and when it opens i know only opens for a short time and they rush out and some of them manage to rush out some of them are unable to rush out man and after they rush out they think they have got out of hell but what happens is that they fall into another hell another different kind of hell and they suffer again again like a fugitive running running and being tortured and all that and once in a blue moon again the gates of that hell open and some of them rush out again then they fall into another hell might be they fall into a hell now which is like a river and that river is like acid they are burned in that in that river and then they keep going from one hell to another and then finally they come back to the hell where they started so you see they undergo both physical torture as well as mental torture so you can imagine if uh any kind of uh you can't find any worse suffering than to be in that hell and the great hell here is described as being of with the iron roof the iron floor and so hot then you can't run out from that that place it's like a oven and this king yamaha even here the buddha says makes a wish that one day he can be reborn as a human being so that he can hear the dhamma the buddha's dharma and so that he can get out of samsara so now we are at this point where we are able to hear the dhamma maybe previously the buddha said that most of us or practically all of us have been through samsara for an extremely long time and most of the rebirth we have gone through maybe at one time we were king yama also maybe at one time we also wish that one day we get birthed as a human being and we might hear the dhamma so now we are at this stage where we are able to hear the dhamma we should not [Music] let the chants go off lightly we should take the trouble to really understand the dharma and practice so that we can get out of hell and never fall into this woeful planes again probably many times you have already fallen to the vocal pain i think some of us now you can see that in our dreams we have been to certain places and like hell so the fact that he can come up in our dreams maybe this is from our memory in our past life we have been in such places
